architecture_summary: Add DistributedLeaseClaimPlanner as a pure receipt planner that hashes the distributed lease policy, scheduled job identity, request payload, backend operation payload, and plan envelope before any lease backend can be claimed. The planner supports sqlite_compare_and_swap, postgres_advisory_lock, redis_redlock, etcd_lease, consul_session, and external_http_gateway operation payloads, but it never executes the operation. Plan-only receipts preserve a non-live lease claim plan, dry-run receipts reject live claim evidence, and claim-approved receipts only admit an external claim when operator approval, adapter claim receipt, 2xx response status, response hash, observed payload hash matching the expected job hash, future lease expiry, and fencing token evidence when policy requires fencing are present. The receipt schema records no-local-lease-service-call, no-authentication, and no-secret flags and is indexed in the governance protocol manifest for public validation. Temporal lease-window receipts remain the downstream runtime admission proof after a distributed claim has been externally granted.
data_model_changes: add DistributedLeaseBoundaryPolicy for backend kind, service id, endpoint, default and max lease durations, fencing-token requirement, receipt requirement, and metadata
add DistributedLeaseJob for job id, target, attempt, expected payload hash, idempotency key, scheduler receipt ref, and metadata

add DistributedLeaseClaimBoundaryRequest for tenant, actor, command, worker, mode, runtime time, readiness evidence refs, approval refs, adapter claim receipt refs, response refs, observed payload hash, lease expiry, fencing token, and metadata

add DistributedLeaseClaimReceipt for backend identity, policy hash, job identity, request payload hash, operation payload hash, plan hash, approval and adapter evidence, response evidence, observed payload hash, lease expiry, fencing token, claim outcome, blocked reasons, required actions, required controls, non-live execution flags, and receipt hash
rollback_plan: Remove DistributedLeaseClaimPlanner, its receipt schema, protocol manifest entry, protocol document count and rule update, focused tests, proof coverage matrix surface and generated fixture/doc changes, and SDLC evidence artifacts, then rerun schema validation, protocol manifest validation, public repository surface validation, proof coverage matrix validation, focused distributed lease claim tests, SDLC artifact validation, security review validation, release readiness validation, and workspace governance preflight.
migration_plan: No persistence, credential, scheduler, worker, distributed lease backend, deployment, external endpoint, or runtime route migration is required. The change adds a local deterministic planner and schema only.
